A job message must be an exported named struct: generated code refers to
it as a package-qualified type, so an unexported type is rejected at
generation.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26

require github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs v0.0.0

replace github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs => JOBSDIR
-- main.go --
package main

func main() {
	if err := run(); err != nil {
		panic(err)
	}
}
-- shared/shared.go --
package shared

import "github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/jobs"

//fabrik:provider
func NewJobStore() (jobs.Store, error) { return jobs.NewMemoryStore(), nil }

//fabrik:hook start
func Boot(mgr *jobs.Manager) error { return nil }
-- mail/mail.go --
package mail

import "context"

type welcome struct{ UserID int64 }

//fabrik:job
func SendWelcome(ctx context.Context, m welcome) error { return nil }
-- want/diags --
error: mail/mail.go:7:1: handler SendWelcome: the message type must be an exported named struct
  help: generated code refers to it as a package-qualified type; name it and capitalize
